L1.area: HTTP request handling and file serving logic

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied path values in file operation requests are not sanitized for directory traversal sequences, allowing a remote attacker to access or write files outside the intended storage directory.

L3.area: File upload and download HTTP request handlers
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied path values and request URIs in file upload and download requests are not sanitized for directory traversal sequences (such as '..'), allowing a remote attacker to write uploaded files to or read files from arbitrary locations outside the intended storage directory.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied path parameters in file upload requests and request URIs in download requests are not sanitized for directory traversal sequences, allowing attackers to read or write files outside the intended storage directory.
